**Corollary (3.4):** If $G_2$ is trivial and $G_0$ is generated by elements whose images in $G_1$ under $\theta_1$ are $\alpha_1, \alpha_2, \dots$, then $\omega_1$ is onto and its kernel is the consequence of $\{\alpha_1, \alpha_2, \dots\}$.

*Proof.* Take $G_2 = 1$, so the presentation of $G_2$ is trivial. The images $\bar{\theta}_2 z_k$ in $F_2$ are trivial, so the added relators reduce to $\bar{\theta}_1 z_k$. The van Kampen presentation becomes $|\mathbf{x} : \mathbf{r}, \{\bar{\theta}_1 z_k\}|$. Since $\omega_1 G_1$ together with the trivial group generates $G$, $\omega_1$ is onto. The kernel is the consequence of $\mathbf{r}$ and $\{\bar{\theta}_1 z_k\}$, i.e., the consequence of the images of generators of $G_0$ in $G_1$.
